title: bead-close-evidence/v1
description: The evidence a bead cites when it is closed. Every claim a close makes must be traceable to an artifact at the same SHA: proofs, entry-point traces, and live evidence when live behaviour is claimed. Limitations and follow-ups are mandatory fields, not optional politeness, because the failure this contract exists to prevent is a close that is silent about what it did not do.

schema.description: Contract discriminator. Consumers must reject any document whose schema they do not implement rather than best-effort parse it (E_UNSUPPORTED_SCHEMA).

repo.description: Repository this evidence was produced in. The repo name lives in the payload, never in the schema, so the schema file stays byte-identical across repos.

bead_id.description: The bead being closed, e.g. rust-oracledb-evidence-controls-f1cl.1.

scope.description: What this close covers and, explicitly, what it does not. out_of_scope may be empty, but it must be stated.

proofs.description: Evidence documents backing this close. Each must be recorded at source.sha; a proof from an earlier commit does not describe the code being closed (E_STALE_SHA).

integration_evidence.description: How the change was shown to be reachable in the product, not merely unit-tested. entry_points may be empty for changes with no runtime surface (docs, schemas); entry_points_excluded_reason is where that is stated. The tri-state rule that enforces this is defined by the entry-trace control, which imports this schema.

entry_points_excluded_reason.description: Machine-readable token stating why this change has no entry point, e.g. no-runtime-surface.

live_evidence.description: Live or external-system evidence. If claimed is true, at least one artifact is required: a live claim with nothing to point at is the exact failure this contract exists to catch (E_LIVE_CLAIM_WITHOUT_ARTIFACT).

lane.description: Scheduled CI/live lane that produced the artifacts. Required by the post-charter close auditor for live claims.

run_id.description: Immutable scheduled-lane run identifier. Required by the post-charter close auditor for live claims.

limitations.description: Known limitations of this close. Required and may be empty, but empty is then an explicit assertion that there are none.

known_defects.description: Defects known at close time. Each must name an open bead; a known defect with nowhere to be fixed is how a defect disappears (E_DEFECT_WITHOUT_BEAD).

follow_ups.description: Bead ids carrying the deferred work.

readiness.description: What this close claims, and on what basis. The pair is checked: some bases cannot support a ready claim.

basis.description: required-proof: a full Required graph proof at this SHA. live-evidence: an exact-SHA live artifact. scoped-test: a targeted test over part of the change; real evidence, but never sufficient to call a bead ready (E_SCOPED_TEST_CANNOT_MARK_READY). manual-review: read, not executed; cannot support ready (E_INSUFFICIENT_READINESS_BASIS).

schema.description: Which evidence contract the referenced document satisfies.

trace.description: The test or artifact tracing this entry point through to a dispatch/result.

bead_id.description: Open bead tracking this defect, or null. Nullable on purpose: an untracked defect must be representable so it can be rejected with a precise reason (E_DEFECT_WITHOUT_BEAD) rather than silently omitted from the list.

sha.description: The commit this artifact was recorded for.

sha1.description: Full 40-character lowercase hex git object name. Abbreviated or uppercase SHAs are rejected: evidence must name an exact, unambiguous commit.

sourceRef.description: The exact tree this evidence was produced from.

tree_clean.description: False means uncommitted changes were present. Evidence from a dirty tree is not reproducible and is rejected (E_TREE_DIRTY).
